title: Nginx returns 502 Bad Gateway under load
symptoms: Nginx error log shows 'upstream prematurely closed connection'
502 rate spikes in sync with request volume
root_cause: keepalive_requests on upstream exhausted and Nginx re-opens connections aggressively
Backend timeout is shorter than Nginx proxy_read_timeout
diagnosis: tail -f /var/log/nginx/error.log | grep upstream
ss -s on the Nginx host - look at TIME_WAIT count
Compare upstream_response_time vs request_time
fix: Tune upstream keepalive:
Ensure backend keepalive timeout > Nginx keepalive_timeout
prevention: Load test before launch and alert on 5xx > 1%
Emit upstream_response_time to Prometheus
Use circuit breakers in the application tier

title: Intermittent DNS resolution timeouts inside the cluster
symptoms: Random 5s latency spikes on outbound calls
Issue disappears for a while after a redeploy
root_cause: conntrack race in the Linux kernel for parallel UDP DNS lookups
ndots:5 default forces unnecessary search domain lookups
diagnosis: kubectl -n kube-system logs deploy/coredns | grep -i error
Use `dig +tries=1 +time=2` from inside a pod repeatedly
Check conntrack: `conntrack -L -p udp | wc -l`
fix: Set dnsPolicy ClusterFirst and tune ndots in the pod spec:

```yaml
dnsConfig:
  options:
    - name: ndots
      value: "2"
    - name: single-request-reopen
```
Deploy NodeLocal DNSCache to avoid conntrack races
Scale CoreDNS replicas to >= nodes/4
prevention: Bake DNS latency SLOs into your CI smoke tests
Monitor coredns_dns_response_rcode_count_total
Upgrade kernel to one with conntrack fix

title: Nginx upstream timeout causing 504 errors
symptoms: Upstream response time exceeds configured timeout
root_cause: proxy_read_timeout too short for backend processing
Backend application slow due to database queries
Network latency between Nginx and backend
diagnosis: Check nginx error logs for upstream timeout errors
Monitor upstream response time in nginx stats
Test backend response time directly
Review nginx proxy timeout configuration
fix: Increase proxy_read_timeout and proxy_connect_timeout values
Optimize backend application performance
Scale upstream servers horizontally
Implement caching at nginx level
prevention: Set appropriate timeout values based on backend performance
Monitor upstream response times and set alerts
Load test backend capacity
Use connection pooling for upstream servers

title: SSL handshake failures causing connection errors
symptoms: Clients receiving SSL handshake failed errors
root_cause: SSL certificate expired or not yet valid
diagnosis: Check SSL certificate validity: openssl x509 -in cert.pem -noout -dates
Test SSL handshake: openssl s_client -connect host:443
Review server SSL configuration
Check browser developer tools for SSL errors
fix: Renew or replace expired SSL certificate
Install complete certificate chain including intermediates
Update SSL configuration to support modern TLS versions
Configure appropriate cipher suites
prevention: Set up certificate expiry monitoring and alerts
Use Let's Encrypt with automatic renewal
Regularly test SSL configuration
Keep SSL libraries and protocols updated

title: DNS resolution failures preventing service access
symptoms: DNS queries timing out or returning NXDOMAIN
Some hosts resolving while others do not
root_cause: DNS cache poisoning or incorrect records
diagnosis: Test DNS resolution: nslookup or dig
Check DNS server configuration
Verify network connectivity to DNS servers
Review DNS cache and records
fix: Fix DNS server configuration or restart DNS service
Update client DNS configuration
Clear DNS cache: ipconfig /flushdns (Windows) or systemd-resolve --flush-caches (Linux)
Check and fix DNS records
prevention: Use multiple DNS servers for redundancy
Monitor DNS server health and response times
Implement DNS caching resolvers
Document DNS configuration standards

title: Firewall rules blocking legitimate traffic
symptoms: Some clients can connect while others cannot
root_cause: Rule order causing traffic to be blocked
Missing allow rules for required services
diagnosis: Check firewall status and rules: iptables -L -n or firewall-cmd --list-all
Test connectivity with firewall disabled
Review firewall logs for blocked connections
Audit firewall rule configuration
fix: Add allow rules for required services and ports
Fix rule order to allow legitimate traffic
Remove overly restrictive rules
Reload firewall configuration
prevention: Document firewall rules and their purpose
Use firewall management tools for consistency
Test firewall changes in staging environment
Implement firewall rule review process

title: Load balancer health checks failing causing traffic issues
symptoms: Load balancer marking healthy servers as unhealthy
503 errors when all backends marked unhealthy
Health check passing locally but failing from load balancer
root_cause: Health check endpoint incorrect or not responding
Network issues between load balancer and backend
diagnosis: Check load balancer health check configuration
Test health check endpoint from load balancer
Review backend server logs for health check requests
Monitor health check success rate
fix: Fix health check endpoint path and response
Adjust health check interval and timeout values
Fix network connectivity between load balancer and backend
Implement proper health check logic in application
prevention: Design robust health check endpoints
Test health checks from load balancer perspective
Monitor health check metrics and set alerts
Document health check requirements

title: High network latency causing application performance issues
symptoms: Database queries taking longer than expected
diagnosis: Measure network latency: ping and traceroute
Check bandwidth utilization with network monitoring tools
Review network equipment logs
Analyze network path and routing
fix: Increase network bandwidth or optimize traffic
Optimize network routing or use CDN
Fix or replace faulty network equipment
Deploy services closer to users or use regional deployments
prevention: Monitor network latency and bandwidth utilization
Use network performance monitoring tools
Implement traffic shaping and QoS
Design for regional deployment to reduce latency

title: Port conflicts preventing services from starting
symptoms: Service fails to start with port already in use error
Only one instance of service can run
Port already bound by another process
root_cause: Another process already using the port
Previous instance of service not properly shut down
Port not released after service crash
diagnosis: Check what process is using the port: netstat -tulpn | grep <port> or lsof -i :<port>
Review service configuration for port settings
Check for zombie processes
Review system logs for service shutdown errors
fix: Kill the process using the port: kill -9 <pid>
Change service configuration to use different port
Properly stop previous service instance
Implement proper service shutdown procedures
prevention: Use unique ports for different services
Document port assignments
Implement service discovery for port management
Use process managers for proper service lifecycle
